name: Wind-Up
description: Wind-Up is a wind-up robot who is one of the Tech core Skylanders in Skylanders: Swap Force. He is available in the Sheep Wreck Island Adventure pack. A single pack was offered at Gamestop in the US with pre-orders for Skylanders: Trap Team.
biography: Built in the enchanted workshop of a toymaker obsessed with time, Wind-Up was created to help keep his massive collection of complicated clocks working perfectly. But when the toymaker popped out of existence in a freak accident caused by putting hot cocoa in a cross-wired Arkeyan oven, Wind-Up found himself surrounded by an invading Cyclops platoon - with an eye towards claiming the toymaker's secrets for themselves. Using split-second timing, clockwork strategy, and his totally wound up energy, Wind-Up bravely sprang into action and handily defeated the Cyclops. He later joined the Skylanders to help swing the pendulum the other way in their fight against anything that threatens Skylands.
personality: Wind-Up is always on time. Bursting with coiled-up energy, he's also always ready to spring into action.

story.content: Early concept art depicts Wind-Up as a gumball-vending machine who could summon an army of small toy minions. His blue parts were originally colored green.

Wind-Up is a blue and gold metal wind-up robot, of short stature. Each of his arms are blue in color with a gold ring on the end, a gold wind up key on top, and his hands consist of silver claws; he also has two metal legs with blue-ankled feet and gold shoe-like toes. His head is blue with a gold bottom jaw, mechanical segmented eyes, and a large key on top. A wind up coil keeps Wind-Up's body connected as a torso.

content: Hmm, better than a new coat of paint!
However you want to spin it!
Success for me, just like clockwork!

trivia: Wind-Up's voice actor, Bob Bergen, is best known for recreating the voice of Porky Pig from the Looney Tunes Show.
Whenever Wind-Up wears a hat, it constantly spins around. This might possibly be because of the wind-up key on his head.
He, Bouncer, Magna Charge, Drill Sergeant, Jawbreaker, Gearshift, and Ro-Bow are the only seven Tech Skylanders to be fully robotic.
However, he is the first one with an organic voice instead of a mechanical type.
He, Jawbreaker, Gearshift and Ro-Bow each have two legs instead of wheels for feet.
His Soul Gem Spring-Loaded Crank adds +15 to speed in Swap Force.
Whichever upgrade path Wind-Up chooses, his original turn key changes into a gyroscope with two gears in it.
He is the only Skylander to be a toy both in game and figure.
Wind-Up's official catchphrase sounds similar to Spyro's ('All Fired Up!'). This also applies to Gearshift ('All Geared Up!').
Wind-Up was one of the many Skylanders planned to appear in Skylanders: Ring of Heroes. Before the game's closure, only concept art of his Awakened Form was released.
In Swap Force, Wind-Up will automatically turn to keep facing nearby enemies.

trailers.name: Skylanders Swap Force - Wind-Up Soul Gem Preview (All Wound Up)

gameplay: Skylanders can use abilities depending on their level. Starting Powers: Spark Punch (Primary Attack): Press (Y) to perform meelee punch. When the character is wound up, this creates an extra spark that does more damage. Wind up by holding (A). Gear Summon (Secondary Attack): Press (X) to summon a gear under enemies that will damage them. When wound up, gears will do more damage and throw enemies into the air. Wind up by holding (A). Upgrades: Boxing Glove (requires level: 3): Hold and release (Y) to attack with a giant boxing glove. When the character is wound up, this does more damage and has a large knockback. Wind up by holding (A). Wacky Wind (requires level: 6): While sprinting (A), Wind-Up will spin his top half around, damaging enemies that come too close. 3DSupgrade3 (requires level: 9): 3DSupgrade3desc

basic.name: Short Circuit
basic.content: Press Attack 1 to attack nearby enemies with sparking claws.

basic.name: Wind Up!
basic.content: Press Attack 2 to wind up and damage nearby enemies. Winding up is needed to perform more powerful attacks.

basic_upgrades.name: Overcrank Stabilizer
basic_upgrades.content: Press Attack 2 to rapidly fill the wind up meter. When full, press Attack 2 again to overcrank. During overcrank press Attack 1 to cause a large explosion that damages nearby enemies.

basic_upgrades.name: Winding Weapon
basic_upgrades.content: Press Attack 2 to wind up, dealing increased damage and pulling enemies in.

basic_upgrades.name: Spring Shot
basic_upgrades.content: Press Attack 3 to shoot a spring shot that launches enemies into the air. Attacking launched enemies will deal automatic critical damage!

basic_upgrades.name: Power Pistons
basic_upgrades.content: Press Attack 1 to deal increased punching damage.

soulgems.name: Spring-Loaded Crank
soulgems.content: Speed is increased. Automatically wind up while running.

abilities.name: Cymbals
abilities.content: Hold Attack 1 to charge a cymbal attack, release to attack all enemies in the way with a pair of cymbals.

abilities.name: Crash Cymbals
abilities.content: Cymbal attacks do increased damage. An easy way to crash any party!

abilities.name: Toy Gun
abilities.content: Hold Attack 3 to shoot rapid fire suction cup projectiles at enemies.

abilities.name: Windup Punch
abilities.content: Hold Attack 1 to charge a boxing glove punch, release to deliver a massive punch to enemies.

abilities.name: Auto Wind Up
abilities.content: Taking damage automatically adds to winding up.

abilities.name: Power Crank
abilities.content: Press Attack 2 rapidly to begin overcranking which now pulls enemies in and deals bonus damage.
